header.title: Daily FI Tape - Market Briefing
header.as_of_note: Session briefing with Asia / Pre-market shown separately.

bullets: UST yields finished the session modestly higher with the long end leading the move.
Credit spreads stayed contained, keeping the move framed as rates-led rather than stress-led.
The dollar held firm into the session close, reinforcing the cross-asset read-through.
overall_summary.ny_close_causal_narrative: The session narrative was a rates-led bear-steepening supported by supply concession and policy-sensitive repricing, while credit stress remained contained.

top_drivers.title: Long-end supply concession
top_drivers.short_summary: The 10Y sector underperformed as the session priced more term-premium concession.
top_drivers.detail: The move links to supply absorption rather than a late-session risk shock.

top_drivers.title: Policy-sensitive repricing
top_drivers.short_summary: Front-end rates rose modestly as the path of easing stayed conditional.
top_drivers.detail: The move was directional but not disorderly, leaving the curve steeper into close.

top_drivers.title: Contained credit stress
top_drivers.short_summary: Tighter IG spreads kept the session from reading as broad risk-off.
top_drivers.detail: Credit resilience narrowed the interpretation toward rates and supply rather than funding stress.

latest_asia_premarket.summary: Separately, early Asia trading showed slightly firmer Treasury futures and a softer dollar tone before the Taipei cutoff.

long_thesis.english_markdown:
# Market Thesis

## Session Narrative

UST yields ended the session modestly higher as the curve bear-steepened, the dollar held firm, and credit spreads stayed contained. The session read stays rates-led rather than broad stress-led.

## Market Drivers

1. Long-end supply concession: The 10Y sector underperformed as the session priced more term-premium concession.
2. Policy-sensitive repricing: Front-end rates rose modestly as the path of easing stayed conditional.
3. Contained credit stress: Tighter IG spreads kept the session from reading as broad risk-off.

## Latest Asia / Pre-market Update

Separately, early Asia trading showed steadier Treasury futures and a softer dollar tone before the Taipei 08:00 cutoff. This update is a separate forward-looking market check.

## Market Watch

- US retail sales (2026-01-16): A demand surprise could affect the front-end rates path and dollar direction.
- Treasury coupon supply (2026-01-20): Auction demand will test whether the latest term-premium concession is sufficient.

methodology_note: Synthesized market analysis from market levels and reporting context. Asia and pre-market developments are separated from the close narrative.
